problem.name: HSTU_Pet_Recommendation
problem.description:
You are an expert ML engineer tasked with improving an HSTU (Hierarchical Sequential Transduction Unit) sequential recommendation model on the Pet domain. The model uses SiLU-based attention with L2-normalized embeddings and sampled softmax loss.

Hardware / Runtime Requirements:
- CPU is acceptable (no GPU required). Use device handling (e.g., device = torch.device('cpu'); model.to(device); tensor.to(device)).
- Training time is limited to ~30 minutes per epoch. Keep model changes efficient.

Libraries Allowed:
- You may use any libraries that can be imported. (Libraries List: numpy, scipy, pandas, torch, scikit-learn, networkx, transformers, sentence-transformers, regex, RapidFuzz, matplotlib, shapely, omegaconf, PyYAML, openai, requests, tqdm, safetensors)

Model Architecture:
- HSTU uses SiLU-gated attention (no softmax), L2-normalized output embeddings, and temperature-scaled sampled softmax loss.
- Key hyperparameters: hidden_dim, linear_dim, attention_dim, num_blocks, num_heads, dropout, attn_dropout, temperature, num_negatives.

Acceptance Checklist:
- Runs on CPU without OOM errors.
- Training loss decreases and stays finite.
- HR@5 and NDCG@5 metrics improve over the baseline.

initial_idea.title: HSTU: Hierarchical Sequential Transduction Unit for Pet Recommendation
initial_idea.content: The HSTU model applies SiLU-gated attention (a non-softmax attention mechanism) to sequential recommendation. Unlike standard SASRec which uses softmax attention, HSTU uses SiLU activation on attention scores and masks with 0.0 instead of -inf, avoiding the fully-masked-row NaN problem. Output embeddings are L2-normalized and logits are temperature-scaled for stable sampled softmax training. The model is applied to the Amazon Pet dataset with leave-one-out evaluation.
